title: Thismia singeri
taxonomy: Thismia singeri is a species of flowering plant in the family Balsamorhizaceae. It belongs to the genus Thismia, which includes a group of rare and unusual plants. The plant is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. Thismia singeri is often found growing in lowland rainforests and cloud forests. The classification of Thismia singeri as a species of flowering plant within the Balsamorhizaceae family is still a subject of ongoing research and study.
properties: Thismia singeri, a plant species endemic to Borneo, is reported to have potential therapeutic properties. However, there is limited scientific research on the specific medicinal uses of Thismia singeri.
